demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: He woke up in the night making this awful harsh, barking sound like a seal, and every time he cries he makes a squeaky noise trying to breathe in. His voice has gone all croaky. It's been getting worse since it got dark and we're really scared.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on suddenly a few hours ago and it's worse at night — the noisy breathing when he's upset is frightening and we don't know what to do.

symptom_narrative.onset: the barking cough and croaky voice came on suddenly about six hours ago; he'd had a runny, blocked nose for about two days before that
symptom_narrative.duration: the barking cough and noisy breathing for about six hours, worse since nightfall; the runny nose for two days
symptom_narrative.character: a harsh, seal-like barking cough and a squeaky noise when he breathes in, but only when he's crying or upset
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the noise is really frightening when he gets upset, but when we hold him and he calms down he settles and breathes quietly again
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: his throat and chest — the cough and the noisy breathing
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: much worse at night and when he's crying or agitated; quieter when he's calm and resting
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: cuddling him and keeping him calm settles the noise; we tried sitting in a steamy bathroom and it only helped a tiny bit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: crying, getting upset, and lying down at night
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he feels a bit warm, still had his runny nose, and his voice is hoarse; he's still drinking his water
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he's had colds before but never this barking cough or noisy breathing
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he's distressed and hard to settle when he wakes coughing, but calms in our arms

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: gave him a small amount about two hours ago

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: toddler at home with both parents; a cold had been going around the family

objective_data_offered.value: about 37.8 degrees under the arm with our home thermometer
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: axillary reading on a home digital thermometer in an unsettled toddler, single measurement

objective_data_offered.value: he's around 12 kg at his last weigh-in

on video he makes a harsh barking cough and a squeaky noise breathing in when he cries, and you can see his tummy and the skin around his ribs pull in a little while he's crying; when he settles in mum's arms it all quietens down and he breathes quietly
when he's calm and resting he isn't making the squeaky noise and isn't struggling; he's alert, looking around, and grabbing at his drink bottle
he's swallowing his own spit normally and drinking sips, he's not drooling, and his lips and colour look normal to us, not pale or blue
he's not sitting hunched forward or refusing to lie back, and he can still cry loudly
